TIA.name: TIA
TIA.description: The younger sister. Bright-eyed, dangerously curious, and hiding more under the surface than anyone suspects.

EVA.name: EVA
EVA.description: The older sister. Sharp tongue, sharper instincts. She'd burn the city down to keep Tia safe.

VEIL.name: VEIL [ENCRYPTED]
VEIL.description: A corporate shadow. No face, no name, just orders. But even shadows have agendas.

KANE.name: KANE [HOLO LINK]
KANE.description: Your handler. Calm voice, steady hands. The kind of man who tells you to run exactly two seconds before the explosion.

ELARA.name: ELARA
ELARA.description: A ghost in the machine. Part human, part prototype. She shouldn't exist—but she remembers everything.

storyDialogue.text: Runner, you online? I'm picking up some weird signal echoes in Sector 7. Not interference—something's broadcasting.
Corporate logs say the district's been dark for three years. No power, no people, no reason for a signal. So why is something pinging?
Disregard the signal. Your assignment hasn't changed, Runner. The sisters are your only priority.
Veil's been... twitchy about this sector. More than usual. Just keep your eyes open, yeah?
[From a rooftop above] You're making a lot of noise for someone sneaking through a dead zone, Corp.
Don't bother reaching for it. I already disabled your sidearm. Remotely. Three blocks back.
Eva, wait! He's... different from the last ones they sent. I can feel it.
You 'feel it' about every stray we find, Tia. That's how we almost got flatlined in New Shibuya.
This is different! He's not even fighting back. Look at him—he's listening for the signal too.
[Static] ...nner, I'm losing y—the signal is overriding our encryp— ...it knows you're there.
...What was that? Your handler just got scrambled. That doesn't happen to Corp-grade comms.
The signal—it's coming from below us. Beneath the old rail line. Something's alive down there.
Alright, Corp. You want answers? So do we. But if you try anything, I will end you. Non-negotiable.
This tunnel... it wasn't on any city map. Someone built this and erased it.
Erased it from public records, sure. But I bet Veil knows exactly what's down here. That's why he sent you.
Wait— do you hear that? It sounds like... breathing? No... it's data. Compressed, looping, alive.
...Hello.
I've been waiting. Not for you specifically—for anyone. I've been alone down here for 1,094 days.
[Drawing weapon] What the hell— She's... half machine. Tia, get behind me.
No, Eva. Look at her eyes. She's scared. She's more scared than we are.
I am Project Elara. Prototype neural-organic hybrid. Serial number redacted. Status... discontinued.
They built me to process the city's neural grid. But when I started dreaming, they decided I was a malfunction.
The signal you followed? That was me. Calling out. Over and over. Hoping someone would hear.
Runner. I see your vitals have spiked. Tell me—what did you find in the tunnels?
Silence? Interesting. You've been in contact with the sisters, haven't you? And... something else?
[Whispered] Runner, listen carefully. Veil knows about Elara. He always knew. She's the real target. The sisters were bait.
Project Elara was worth billions before they buried her. A self-evolving AI in a human shell? Corps would go to war for that.
[Intercepting comms] We heard ALL of that, Corp. Your boss wants to strip-mine a girl for parts. Sound familiar?
She's not a project. She's a person! She dreams, she's scared, she—she asked us our names!
Tia's right. For once, I agree. Runner—you've got a choice to make. Right now.
You either help us protect her... or we treat you like every other Corp dog that's come for us.
You don't have to choose me. I understand what I am—a liability. I always have been.
But if you do choose... I can help. I can see every camera, every lock, every patrol route in this city. All of it. At once.
Runner... whatever you decide, I'm with you. I didn't sign up to dismantle a kid. Even a synthetic one.
Runner. Your silence has been noted. Extraction team deployed. You have four minutes.
Four minutes. Cute. They don't know this district like we do. Move.
Elara, can you kill the lights in blocks 4 through 7? And loop the surveillance on the north corridor?
Already done. I also rerouted three patrol drones into each other. You're welcome.
...Okay, I'm starting to like this one.
Runner, I've opened an exit route through the old monorail. It leads out past the corp perimeter. Go now.
[Fading signal] You're making a mistake, Runner. There is no world where this ends well for you.
We made it... We actually made it. [Laughing] Eva, we made it!
Don't celebrate yet. Veil doesn't forget. And Corps don't forgive. But... yeah. We made it.
The sky. I've seen it in data, in satellite imagery, in a million photographs. But I've never felt it before.
Is it always this... vast?
Yeah. It is. And it's yours now too.
So, Runner. You're burned with the Corps. Hunted. No name, no backup, no way back.
Welcome to our side. Try not to die on the first day.
[Encrypted] Runner... I wiped your file from the Corp database. You're a ghost now. Stay alive out there.
Thank you. All of you. For hearing me.
